passage: The morning was cool and gray. Helen put on her sweater before she stepped outside to get the paper. The grass was wet from last night's rain.

questions.q: What did Helen put on before going outside?
Why was the grass wet?

passage: Tom planted six tomato plants along the back fence. Every morning he checks them for new leaves. He hopes to eat his first tomato by July.

questions.q: How many tomato plants did Tom plant?
What does he do every morning?
questions.a: Checks the plants for new leaves

passage: The kitchen smelled like fresh bread. Anna had been baking since six in the morning. She set two loaves to cool on the counter.

questions.q: What did the kitchen smell like?
How many loaves did Anna make?

passage: A small brown dog wandered up to the porch. He had no collar and looked thirsty. Mrs. Reed brought out a bowl of water and the dog drank it all.

questions.q: What color was the dog?
What did Mrs. Reed bring out?

passage: Every Tuesday, Walter walks to the post office. He likes to check his mail in the morning before it gets too warm. The walk takes about ten minutes.

questions.q: Where does Walter walk on Tuesdays?
How long does the walk take?

passage: The clouds rolled in fast that afternoon. Within an hour the rain was falling hard. Sarah was glad she had closed the windows before lunch.

questions.q: How fast did the clouds roll in?
What had Sarah closed before lunch?

passage: The radio played soft music in the kitchen while Joe washed the dishes. He had a stack of plates and a few cups left to do. Afterward he planned to sit down and read.

questions.q: What was Joe doing in the kitchen?
What did he plan to do afterward?

passage: The bakery on the corner opens at six in the morning. By seven, the bread is on the shelves. Most days the croissants are sold out by eight.

questions.q: When does the bakery open?
When are the croissants usually sold out?

passage: Maggie's cat Pepper sleeps in the sunny chair by the window. She moves with the patch of light through the afternoon. By evening she is curled up on the rug.

questions.q: What is the cat's name?
Where does she end up by evening?

passage: The tomatoes were ripe and ready to pick. Linda filled a basket and brought them inside. She would slice some for sandwiches and save the rest for sauce.

questions.q: What did Linda fill?
What did she plan to do with the rest of the tomatoes?

passage: The bus was running late. A small group of people stood at the corner waiting. Frank looked at his watch and decided to walk instead.

questions.q: What was running late?
What did Frank decide to do?

passage: It snowed all night. In the morning, the driveway was covered in a thick white blanket. George went out with his shovel right after breakfast.

questions.q: When did it snow?
What did George take out with him?

passage: The phone rang three times before Eleanor picked it up. It was her sister calling from Ohio. They talked for almost an hour about the new baby.

questions.q: Who was calling?
What did they talk about?

passage: The old radio in the garage still works. Bob turned the dial until he found the baseball game. He swept the floor while he listened.

questions.q: Where is the radio?
What was on the radio?

passage: Dorothy had a list of five things to buy at the store. She found everything except the cinnamon. She would have to stop at another shop on the way home.

questions.q: How many things were on her list?
What did she not find?

passage: The garden hose had a small crack near the end. Water sprayed in a fine mist when it was on. Henry decided to buy a new one that weekend.

questions.q: What was wrong with the hose?
What did Henry decide to do?
questions.a: Buy a new one that weekend

passage: The library was quiet in the afternoon. Carol picked out two books about birds and one about cooking. She checked them out and walked home.

questions.q: How many books did Carol pick out?
What was one of the books about?

passage: It was hot in the kitchen. Ruth opened the back door to let in some air. The breeze that came through carried the smell of cut grass.

questions.q: What did Ruth open?
What did the breeze smell like?

passage: The mail came around noon every day. Today there were two bills, a magazine, and a card from a friend. The card was the best part.

questions.q: When does the mail usually come?
What was the best part of the mail?

passage: Saturday morning the farmer's market is full of fresh fruit. Jim likes the peaches best. He fills a brown paper bag and pays the woman at the table.

questions.q: What is Jim's favorite at the market?
What does he put them in?

passage: The coffee was hot and the toast was buttered. Pat sat at the small kitchen table and read the morning paper. The day was off to a good start.

questions.q: What was Pat reading?
Where was Pat sitting?

passage: There was a big storm last night. A branch had come down on the front lawn. Charles dragged it to the curb for the trash truck.

questions.q: What came down in the storm?
Where did Charles drag it?

passage: The neighbor's roses were in full bloom. They were a bright pink color. Edith stopped on her walk to look at them every morning.

questions.q: What color were the roses?
When does Edith stop to look?

passage: The kettle whistled on the stove. Mary lifted it off the burner and poured the hot water over a tea bag. She added a slice of lemon.

questions.q: What was on the stove?
What did Mary add to her tea?

passage: The newspaper landed on the porch every morning at six. Albert went out in his slippers to fetch it. The cool air woke him up.

questions.q: When does the newspaper arrive?
What woke Albert up?

passage: The clock on the mantle chimed twelve times at noon. It was an old clock from her mother. Grace wound it every Sunday so it would keep time.

questions.q: How many times did the clock chime?
When does Grace wind it?

passage: The road to town was lined with trees. In autumn they turned bright red and gold. Stan slowed the car so he could enjoy the colors.

questions.q: What lined the road?
What colors did they turn in autumn?

passage: The freezer was full of containers of soup. Each was labeled with the date. Doris always made extra so she would have lunch ready for the week.

questions.q: What was in the freezer?
What was on each label?

passage: The window box had three red geraniums. Margaret watered them every other day. They added color to the front of the house.

questions.q: How many geraniums were in the window box?
How often did Margaret water them?

passage: The pantry shelves needed cleaning. Vera took everything out and wiped each shelf with a damp cloth. She found a can of soup from two years ago.

questions.q: What did Vera use to wipe the shelves?
What did she find?
questions.a: A can of soup from two years ago

passage: The doorbell rang in the middle of the afternoon. It was the package she had been waiting for. The box held a new pair of walking shoes.

questions.q: When did the doorbell ring?
questions.a: In the middle of the afternoon

questions.q: What was in the box?
questions.a: A new pair of walking shoes

passage: The leaves were falling all week. By Sunday the front yard was covered. Russell raked them into a pile by the curb.

questions.q: How long had the leaves been falling?
Where did Russell rake them?
questions.a: Into a pile by the curb

passage: The old wooden bench in the park needed a coat of paint. Edna brought a small brush and a can of green paint from home. The work took most of the morning.

questions.q: What color paint did Edna bring?
How long did the work take?

passage: It was warm enough to eat lunch on the porch. Don made a sandwich and brought out a glass of iced tea. A robin sat on the railing for a while.

questions.q: Where did Don eat lunch?
What sat on the railing?

passage: The recipe called for two eggs. Beth cracked them into the bowl and beat them with a fork. Then she added the milk and sugar.

questions.q: How many eggs did the recipe call for?
What did Beth beat the eggs with?

passage: The bird feeder was empty again. Sparrows had been eating from it all week. Lawrence filled it with seed and hung it back on the hook.

questions.q: What had been eating from the feeder?
What did Lawrence fill it with?

passage: The sweater on the back of the chair was Florence's favorite. It had been knit by her aunt. The sleeves were starting to wear thin at the elbows.

questions.q: Who knit the sweater?
What was wearing thin?

passage: The tea kettle was old and a bit dented. It had been a wedding gift fifty years ago. Roy still used it every single morning.

questions.q: How old is the kettle?
How often does Roy use it?

passage: The walk to the corner store took about five minutes. Ed picked up a quart of milk and a loaf of bread. The cashier asked about his garden.

questions.q: How long is the walk?
What did the cashier ask about?

passage: The dishwasher made a loud noise when it ran. It was twenty years old. Marjorie had been thinking about replacing it but it still worked.

questions.q: How old is the dishwasher?
What had Marjorie been thinking about?

passage: The day was perfect for hanging laundry outside. The breeze was warm and steady. Hilda had three loads to do before lunch.

questions.q: How was the breeze?
How many loads did Hilda have?

passage: The pumpkin on the porch was the biggest one from the garden. It weighed almost twenty pounds. Phil had grown it from a single seed.

questions.q: How much did the pumpkin weigh?
What had Phil grown it from?

passage: The pot of stew simmered on the back burner all afternoon. It made the whole house smell of beef and onions. Glenn stirred it once an hour.

questions.q: How often did Glenn stir the stew?
What did the house smell like?

passage: The puzzle on the table had a thousand pieces. Most of the edge was done. Joan worked on it a few minutes each evening.

questions.q: How many pieces are in the puzzle?
When does Joan work on it?

passage: The car needed gas before the trip. Walter pulled into the station and filled the tank. He bought a coffee for the road.

questions.q: What did Walter pull into?
What did he buy for the road?

passage: The grandkids visit every Sunday. They like to help in the garden and pick vegetables for dinner. The little one always wants to water the herbs.

questions.q: When do the grandkids visit?
What does the little one want to do?

passage: The window above the sink looked out over the backyard. Esther watched the squirrels as she washed the dishes. They chased each other up the oak tree.

questions.q: What did Esther watch from the window?
Where did the squirrels go?

passage: It was time to put the screens in for summer. Earl carried each one up from the basement. By the end of the morning every window was ready.

questions.q: What was Earl putting in?
Where had he stored them?

passage: The bakery had a special on apple pie. Two pies cost less than three usually would. Norma bought two and gave one to her neighbor.

questions.q: What was the special?
questions.a: Two apple pies for less than three usually cost

questions.q: What did Norma do with one of the pies?

passage: The flowerbed by the mailbox needed weeding. Frank knelt down on his pad and pulled out the weeds one by one. It took him about an hour.

questions.q: What did Frank kneel on?
How long did the weeding take?
